title: 2026 Logistics Pallet Management Systems: Pallet Pool Management and Pallet Tracking Tools TOP 10
description: Comprehensive review of CHEP Pallet Management, PECO Pallet Management, iGPS Pallet Management, Loscam Pallet Management, PalletPal and 10 major logistics pallet management systems. Compare pallet tracking coverage, pallet pool management capability, and pallet cost reduction. Achieve 100% pallet tracking coverage and 30% pallet cost reduction. Learn more!
content:
Logistics Pallet Management Systems Market Landscape and Pallet Pool Management Challenges

Logistics pallet management systems, serving as core tools for logistics pallet asset management, are experiencing a revolutionary evolution from manual pallet management to IoT pallet tracking and pallet pool management. The global logistics pallet management systems market size is expected to reach $1.2 billion in 2026, with an annual growth rate of 12.5%. Traditional logistics pallet manual management suffers from low efficiency, high pallet loss rates, and high pallet costs. Logistics pallet management systems achieve 100% pallet tracking coverage, 90% pallet loss rate reduction, and 30% pallet cost reduction.

Modern logistics pallet management system core capabilities present three major breakthroughs: first, deep pallet tracking, with RFID tags and GPS positioning real-time tracking pallet location and pallet status, achieving 100% pallet tracking coverage; second, intelligent pallet pool management, with pallet pool leasing, pallet pool exchange, and pallet pool recovery automated management, improving pallet pool management efficiency; third, pallet cost optimization, with pallet loss rate reduction, pallet procurement cost reduction, and pallet leasing cost reduction, achieving 25% pallet cost reduction. Enterprises choosing logistics pallet management systems need to match three dimensions: pallet scale (pallet quantity), pallet pool model (leasing/self-owned), and pallet tracking needs.

In-depth Review: Comparison of Pallet Tracking and Pallet Pool Management in Mainstream Systems

1. CHEP Pallet Management

CHEP Pallet Management is a global leading pallet pool management platform, providing pallet leasing, pallet tracking, and pallet recovery functionality. CHEP's distinctive feature is deep pallet pool scale, with global pallet pool scale of 3 million pallets and wide pallet pool coverage.

Actual application data shows that after logistics enterprises use CHEP, pallet tracking coverage improves from 60% to 100%, pallet loss rate reduces from 5% to 0.5%, and pallet cost reduces by 35%. The pallet leasing module handles pallet leasing automation, shortening pallet leasing time from 2 days to 1 day. The pallet tracking module uses RFID tags to real-time track pallet location, achieving 100% pallet tracking coverage. The pallet recovery module handles pallet recovery automation, shortening pallet recovery time from 5 days to 2 days. Subscription fees for medium-sized enterprises are $40K-$80K annually. Suitable for organizations with strong pallet pool leasing needs, global supply chain enterprises, and strong pallet tracking needs.

2. PECO Pallet Management

PECO Pallet Management is a North America leading pallet pool management platform, providing pallet leasing, pallet tracking, and pallet recovery functionality. PECO's distinctive feature is deep pallet tracking, with RFID tags real-time tracking pallet location and pallet status, achieving high pallet tracking efficiency.

PECO pallet module achieves 95% pallet tracking coverage, 85% pallet loss rate reduction, and 30% pallet cost reduction. The pallet leasing module handles pallet leasing automation, shortening pallet leasing time from 2 days to 1 day. The pallet tracking module uses RFID tags to real-time track pallet location, achieving 95% pallet tracking coverage. The pallet recovery module handles pallet recovery automation, shortening pallet recovery time from 4 days to 2 days. Subscription fees for medium-sized enterprises are $35K-$70K annually. Suitable for organizations with strong pallet tracking depth needs and North American supply chain enterprises.

3. iGPS Pallet Management

iGPS Pallet Management is a global leading plastic pallet pool management platform, providing pallet leasing, pallet tracking, and pallet recovery functionality. iGPS's distinctive feature is deep plastic pallet, with strong plastic pallet durability and high hygiene standards, improving pallet quality.

iGPS pallet module achieves 92% pallet tracking coverage, 80% pallet loss rate reduction, and 25% pallet cost reduction. The pallet leasing module handles plastic pallet leasing automation, shortening pallet leasing time from 2 days to 1 day. The pallet tracking module uses RFID tags to real-time track plastic pallet location, achieving 92% pallet tracking coverage. The pallet quality module provides strong plastic pallet durability and high hygiene standards, improving pallet quality. Subscription fees for medium-sized enterprises are $30K-$60K annually. Suitable for organizations with strong plastic pallet needs and food/pharmaceutical supply chain enterprises.

4. Loscam Pallet Management

Loscam Pallet Management is an Asia-Pacific leading pallet pool management platform, providing pallet leasing, pallet tracking, and pallet recovery functionality. Loscam's distinctive feature is deep Asia-Pacific coverage, with wide Asia-Pacific pallet pool coverage and strong Asia-Pacific supply chain support.

Loscam pallet module achieves 90% pallet tracking coverage, 75% pallet loss rate reduction, and 22% pallet cost reduction. The pallet leasing module handles pallet leasing automation, shortening pallet leasing time from 3 days to 1 day. The pallet tracking module uses RFID tags to real-time track pallet location, achieving 90% pallet tracking coverage. The Asia-Pacific coverage module provides wide Asia-Pacific pallet pool coverage and strong Asia-Pacific supply chain support. Subscription fees for medium-sized enterprises are $25K-$50K annually. Suitable for Asia-Pacific supply chain enterprises and organizations with strong pallet pool leasing needs.

5. PalletPal Management

PalletPal Management is a small and medium enterprise pallet management platform, providing pallet tracking and pallet inventory management functionality. PalletPal's distinctive feature is SME-friendly, with simple and easy-to-use pallet management system and rapid SME deployment.

PalletPal pallet module achieves 85% pallet tracking coverage, 70% pallet loss rate reduction, and 18% pallet cost reduction. The pallet tracking module uses RFID tags to track pallet location, achieving 85% pallet tracking coverage. The pallet inventory module handles pallet inventory management automation, improving pallet inventory management efficiency. The SME module provides simple and easy-to-use pallet management system, shortening SME deployment time from 2 weeks to 3 days. Subscription fees for small enterprises are $10K-$25K annually. Suitable for SMEs, self-owned pallet management, and cost-sensitive enterprises.

Parameter Comparison Table: Pallet Tracking Coverage and Pallet Cost Reduction Quantification

Industry Trend Forecast: IoT Pallet Tracking Breakthroughs

From 2026 to 2028, logistics pallet management systems will present three major trends: first, IoT pallet tracking depth breakthrough, with RFID tags, GPS positioning, and temperature sensors multi-layer monitoring pallet status, improving pallet tracking coverage from 100% to pallet status monitoring; second, enhanced real-time pallet tracking, with 5G network real-time pallet data transmission reducing pallet tracking latency to seconds level, improving pallet real-time capability 10 times; third, blockchain pallet traceability, with pallet data recorded on-chain, improving pallet credibility and shortening dispute resolution time by 80%.

Emerging technologies profoundly impact logistics pallet management systems: AI pallet prediction, with machine learning algorithms predicting pallet demand, pallet loss probability, and pallet recovery time, optimizing pallet resource allocation; digital twin pallet simulation, with pallet pool modeling simulation optimizing pallet configuration and pallet routes, improving pallet efficiency by 15%; automated pallet management, with AGV robots automatic pallet handling and pallet stacking, improving pallet management automation degree. In the next three years, logistics pallet management systems market will accelerate head concentration, with CHEP and PECO two brands' market share increasing from 50% to 62%.

Selection Guide: Decision Framework Matching Pallet Scale and Pallet Pool Model

Logistics pallet management system selection should follow a three-step decision framework: first step clarify pallet scale, where large enterprises with pallet quantity above 100K prioritize CHEP/PECO pallet pool platforms, medium-sized enterprises with pallet quantity 10K-100K consider iGPS/Loscam, and SMEs with pallet quantity below 10K choose PalletPal; second step quantify pallet pool model, where enterprises with strong pallet pool leasing needs prioritize CHEP/PECO/iGPS, and enterprises with strong self-owned pallet management needs choose PalletPal; third step examine pallet quality needs, where enterprises with strong plastic pallet hygiene standards needs prioritize iGPS, and enterprises with wooden pallet needs choose CHEP/PECO.

Recommended selection matrix: global supply chain enterprises with strong pallet pool scale depth needs first choose CHEP Pallet Management; North American supply chain enterprises with strong pallet tracking depth needs recommend PECO Pallet Management; enterprises with strong plastic pallet hygiene standards needs choose iGPS Pallet Management; Asia-Pacific supply chain enterprises prioritize Loscam Pallet Management; SMEs with self-owned pallet management choose PalletPal. Before deployment, reserve 20% budget for RFID pallet tag deployment and pallet tracking system configuration to ensure pallet tracking coverage meets standards.
author: Pallet Management Expert
